item.title: [Daily] Skills.sh snapshot (2026-03-08)
item.description: Daily snapshot. Providers: skills.sh All Time skills: 41532 Trending skills: 14956 Hot skills: 2499 Top All Time: find-skills (450604) Top Trending: agent-tools (12341) Top Hot: vercel-react-best-practices (134)

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#1): vercel-react-best-practices
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #1 Installs: 134 Source: vercel-labs/agent-skills Description: React and Next.js performance optimization guidelines from Vercel Engineering. This skill should be used when writing, reviewing, or refactoring React/Next.js code to ensure optimal performance patterns. Triggers on tasks involving React components, Next.js pages, data fetching, bundle optimization, or performance improvements.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#10): systematic-debugging
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #10 Installs: 44 Source: obra/superpowers Description: Use when encountering any bug, test failure, or unexpected behavior, before proposing fixes

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#11): xlsx
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #11 Installs: 36 Source: anthropics/skills Description: Comprehensive spreadsheet creation, editing, and analysis with support for formulas, formatting, data analysis, and visualization. When Claude needs to work with spreadsheets (.xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, .tsv, etc) for: (1) Creating new spreadsheets with formulas and formatting, (2) Reading or analyzing data, (3) Modify existing spreadsheets while preserving formulas, (4) Data analysis and visualization in spreadsheets, or (5) Recalculating formulas

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#12): test-driven-development
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #12 Installs: 36 Source: obra/superpowers Description: Use when implementing any feature or bugfix, before writing implementation code

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#13): subagent-driven-development
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #13 Installs: 31 Source: obra/superpowers Description: Use when executing implementation plans with independent tasks in the current session

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#14): receiving-code-review
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #14 Installs: 28 Source: obra/superpowers Description: Use when receiving code review feedback, before implementing suggestions, especially if feedback seems unclear or technically questionable - requires technical rigor and verification, not performative agreement or blind implementation

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#15): docx
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #15 Installs: 41 Source: anthropics/skills Description: Comprehensive document creation, editing, and analysis with support for tracked changes, comments, formatting preservation, and text extraction. When Claude needs to work with professional documents (.docx files) for: (1) Creating new documents, (2) Modifying or editing content, (3) Working with tracked changes, (4) Adding comments, or any other document tasks

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#16): writing-skills
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #16 Installs: 25 Source: obra/superpowers Description: Use when creating new skills, editing existing skills, or verifying skills work before deployment

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#17): proactive-agent
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #17 Installs: 28 Source: halthelobster/proactive-agent Description: Transform AI agents from task-followers into proactive partners that anticipate needs and continuously improve. Now with WAL Protocol, Working Buffer for context survival, Compaction Recovery, and battle-tested security patterns. Part of the Hal Stack 🦞

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#18): finishing-a-development-branch
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #18 Installs: 25 Source: obra/superpowers Description: Use when implementation is complete, all tests pass, and you need to decide how to integrate the work - guides completion of development work by presenting structured options for merge, PR, or cleanup

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#19): dispatching-parallel-agents
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #19 Installs: 26 Source: obra/superpowers Description: Use when facing 2+ independent tasks that can be worked on without shared state or sequential dependencies

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#2): brainstorming
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #2 Installs: 60 Source: obra/superpowers Description: You MUST use this before any creative work - creating features, building components, adding functionality, or modifying behavior. Explores user intent, requirements and design before implementation.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#20): verification-before-completion
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #20 Installs: 26 Source: obra/superpowers Description: Use when about to claim work is complete, fixed, or passing, before committing or creating PRs - requires running verification commands and confirming output before making any success claims; evidence before assertions always

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#21): webapp-testing
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #21 Installs: 34 Source: anthropics/skills Description: Toolkit for interacting with and testing local web applications using Playwright. Supports verifying frontend functionality, debugging UI behavior, capturing browser screenshots, and viewing browser logs.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#22): using-git-worktrees
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #22 Installs: 26 Source: obra/superpowers Description: Use when starting feature work that needs isolation from current workspace or before executing implementation plans - creates isolated git worktrees with smart directory selection and safety verification

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#23): canvas-design
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #23 Installs: 25 Source: anthropics/skills Description: Create beautiful visual art in .png and .pdf documents using design philosophy. You should use this skill when the user asks to create a poster, piece of art, design, or other static piece. Create original visual designs, never copying existing artists' work to avoid copyright violations.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#24): self-improving-agent
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #24 Installs: 39 Source: charon-fan/agent-playbook Description: A universal self-improving agent that learns from ALL skill experiences. Uses multi-memory architecture (semantic + episodic + working) to continuously evolve the codebase. Auto-triggers on skill completion/error with hooks-based self-correction.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#25): mcp-builder
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #25 Installs: 24 Source: anthropics/skills Description: Guide for creating high-quality MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers that enable LLMs to interact with external services through well-designed tools. Use when building MCP servers to integrate external APIs or services, whether in Python (FastMCP) or Node/TypeScript (MCP SDK).

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#26): doc-coauthoring
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #26 Installs: 22 Source: anthropics/skills Description: Guide users through a structured workflow for co-authoring documentation. Use when user wants to write documentation, proposals, technical specs, decision docs, or similar structured content. This workflow helps users efficiently transfer context, refine content through iteration, and verify the doc works for readers. Trigger when user mentions writing docs, creating proposals, drafting specs, or similar documentation tasks.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#27): theme-factory
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #27 Installs: 21 Source: anthropics/skills Description: Toolkit for styling artifacts with a theme. These artifacts can be slides, docs, reportings, HTML landing pages, etc. There are 10 pre-set themes with colors/fonts that you can apply to any artifact that has been creating, or can generate a new theme on-the-fly.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#28): algorithmic-art
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #28 Installs: 20 Source: anthropics/skills Description: Creating algorithmic art using p5.js with seeded randomness and interactive parameter exploration. Use this when users request creating art using code, generative art, algorithmic art, flow fields, or particle systems. Create original algorithmic art rather than copying existing artists' work to avoid copyright violations.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#29): summarize
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #29 Installs: 18 Source: steipete/clawdis Description: Summarize or extract text/transcripts from URLs, podcasts, and local files (great fallback for “transcribe this YouTube/video”).

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#3): skill-creator
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #3 Installs: 95 Source: anthropics/skills Description: Guide for creating effective skills. This skill should be used when users want to create a new skill (or update an existing skill) that extends Claude's capabilities with specialized knowledge, workflows, or tool integrations.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#30): better-auth-best-practices
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #30 Installs: 18 Source: better-auth/skills Description: Skill for integrating Better Auth - the comprehensive TypeScript authentication framework.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#31): browser-use
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #31 Installs: 17 Source: browser-use/browser-use Description: Automates browser interactions for web testing, form filling, screenshots, and data extraction. Use when the user needs to navigate websites, interact with web pages, fill forms, take screenshots, or extract information from web pages.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#32): pptx
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #32 Installs: 45 Source: anthropics/skills Description: Presentation creation, editing, and analysis. When Claude needs to work with presentations (.pptx files) for: (1) Creating new presentations, (2) Modifying or editing content, (3) Working with layouts, (4) Adding comments or speaker notes, or any other presentation tasks

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#33): web-artifacts-builder
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #33 Installs: 20 Source: anthropics/skills Description: Suite of tools for creating elaborate, multi-component claude.ai HTML artifacts using modern frontend web technologies (React, Tailwind CSS, shadcn/ui). Use for complex artifacts requiring state management, routing, or shadcn/ui components - not for simple single-file HTML/JSX artifacts.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#34): clawhub-skill-vetting
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #34 Installs: 9 Source: hugomrtz/skill-vetting-clawhub Description: Vet ClawHub skills before installation. Use when the user asks about evaluating, auditing, or safely installing OpenClaw/ClawHub skills, or when a skill’s trustworthiness is in question.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#35): vercel-react-native-skills
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #35 Installs: 45 Source: vercel-labs/agent-skills Description: React Native and Expo best practices for building performant mobile apps. Use when building React Native components, optimizing list performance, implementing animations, or working with native modules. Triggers on tasks involving React Native, Expo, mobile performance, or native platform APIs.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#36): characteristic-voice
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #36 Installs: 13 Source: noizai/skills Description: Use this skill whenever the user wants speech to sound more human, companion-like, or emotionally expressive. Triggers include: any mention of 'say like', 'talk like', 'speak like', 'companion voice', 'comfort me', 'cheer me up', 'sound more human', 'good night voice', 'good morning voice', or requests to add fillers, emotion, or personality to generated speech. Also use when the user wants to mimic a specific character's voice, apply speaking style presets (goodnight, morning, comfort, celebration, chatting), tune emotional parameters like warmth or tenderness, or make TTS output feel like a real person talking. If the user asks for a 'voice message', 'companion audio', 'character voice', or wants speech that sighs, laughs, hesitates, or sounds genuinely warm, use this skill. Do NOT use for plain text-to-speech without personality, music generation, sound effects, or general coding tasks unrelated to expressive speech.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#37): seo-audit
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #37 Installs: 39 Source: coreyhaines31/marketingskills Description: When the user wants to audit, review, or diagnose SEO issues on their site. Also use when the user mentions "SEO audit," "technical SEO," "why am I not ranking," "SEO issues," "on-page SEO," "meta tags review," or "SEO health check." For building pages at scale to target keywords, see programmatic-seo. For adding structured data, see schema-markup.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#38): next-best-practices
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #38 Installs: 29 Source: vercel-labs/next-skills Description: Next.js best practices - file conventions, RSC boundaries, data patterns, async APIs, metadata, error handling, route handlers, image/font optimization, bundling

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#39): internal-comms
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #39 Installs: 17 Source: anthropics/skills Description: A set of resources to help me write all kinds of internal communications, using the formats that my company likes to use. Claude should use this skill whenever asked to write some sort of internal communications (status reports, leadership updates, 3P updates, company newsletters, FAQs, incident reports, project updates, etc.).

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#4): using-superpowers
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #4 Installs: 43 Source: obra/superpowers Description: Use when starting any conversation - establishes how to find and use skills, requiring Skill tool invocation before ANY response including clarifying questions

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#40): yahoo-finance
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #40 Installs: 10 Source: gracefullight/stock-checker Description: Get stock prices, quotes, fundamentals, earnings, options, dividends, and analyst ratings using Yahoo Finance. Uses yfinance library - no API key required.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#41): remotion-best-practices
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #41 Installs: 82 Source: remotion-dev/skills Description: Best practices for Remotion - Video creation in React

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#42): competitor-alternatives
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #42 Installs: 22 Source: coreyhaines31/marketingskills Description: When the user wants to create competitor comparison or alternative pages for SEO and sales enablement. Also use when the user mentions 'alternative page,' 'vs page,' 'competitor comparison,' 'comparison page,' '[Product] vs [Product],' '[Product] alternative,' or 'competitive landing pages.' Covers four formats: singular alternative, plural alternatives, you vs competitor, and competitor vs competitor. Emphasizes deep research, modular content architecture, and varied section types beyond feature tables.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#43): okx-dex-market
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #43 Installs: 16 Source: okx/onchainos-skills Description: This skill should be used when the user asks 'what\'s the price of OKB', 'check token price', 'how much is OKB', 'show me the price chart', 'get candlestick data', 'show K-line chart', 'view trade history', 'recent trades for SOL', 'price trend', 'index price', or mentions checking a token\'s current price, viewing price charts, candlestick data, trade history, or historical price trends. Covers real-time on-chain prices, K-line/candlestick charts, trade logs, and index prices across XLayer, Solana, Ethereum, Base, BSC, Arbitrum, Polygon, and 20+ other chains. For token search, market cap, liquidity analysis, trending tokens, or holder distribution, use okx-dex-token instead.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#44): obsidian-bases
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #44 Installs: 12 Source: kepano/obsidian-skills Description: Create and edit Obsidian Bases (.base files) with views, filters, formulas, and summaries. Use when working with .base files, creating database-like views of notes, or when the user mentions Bases, table views, card views, filters, or formulas in Obsidian.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#45): template-skill
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #45 Installs: 8 Source: noizai/skills Description: Reusable template for authoring new Agent Skills with clear triggers, workflow, and I/O contracts.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#46): content-strategy
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #46 Installs: 26 Source: coreyhaines31/marketingskills Description: When the user wants to plan a content strategy, decide what content to create, or figure out what topics to cover. Also use when the user mentions "content strategy," "what should I write about," "content ideas," "blog strategy," "topic clusters," or "content planning." For writing individual pieces, see copywriting. For SEO-specific audits, see seo-audit.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#47): social-content
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #47 Installs: 25 Source: coreyhaines31/marketingskills Description: When the user wants help creating, scheduling, or optimizing social media content for LinkedIn, Twitter/X, Instagram, TikTok, Facebook, or other platforms. Also use when the user mentions 'LinkedIn post,' 'Twitter thread,' 'social media,' 'content calendar,' 'social scheduling,' 'engagement,' or 'viral content.' This skill covers content creation, repurposing, and platform-specific strategies.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#48): pricing-strategy
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #48 Installs: 23 Source: coreyhaines31/marketingskills Description: When the user wants help with pricing decisions, packaging, or monetization strategy. Also use when the user mentions 'pricing,' 'pricing tiers,' 'freemium,' 'free trial,' 'packaging,' 'price increase,' 'value metric,' 'Van Westendorp,' 'willingness to pay,' or 'monetization.' This skill covers pricing research, tier structure, and packaging strategy.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#49): site-architecture
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #49 Installs: 21 Source: coreyhaines31/marketingskills Description: When the user wants to plan, map, or restructure their website's page hierarchy, navigation, URL structure, or internal linking. Also use when the user mentions "sitemap," "site map," "visual sitemap," "site structure," "page hierarchy," "information architecture," "IA," "navigation design," "URL structure," "breadcrumbs," "internal linking strategy," or "website planning." NOT for XML sitemaps (that's technical SEO — see seo-audit). For SEO audits, see seo-audit. For structured data, see schema-markup.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#5): web-design-guidelines
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #5 Installs: 107 Source: vercel-labs/agent-skills Description: Review UI code for Web Interface Guidelines compliance. Use when asked to "review my UI", "check accessibility", "audit design", "review UX", or "check my site against best practices".

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#50): paid-ads
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #50 Installs: 21 Source: coreyhaines31/marketingskills Description: When the user wants help with paid advertising campaigns on Google Ads, Meta (Facebook/Instagram), LinkedIn, Twitter/X, or other ad platforms. Also use when the user mentions 'PPC,' 'paid media,' 'ad copy,' 'ad creative,' 'ROAS,' 'CPA,' 'ad campaign,' 'retargeting,' or 'audience targeting.' This skill covers campaign strategy, ad creation, audience targeting, and optimization.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#6): writing-plans
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #6 Installs: 39 Source: obra/superpowers Description: Use when you have a spec or requirements for a multi-step task, before touching code

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#7): requesting-code-review
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #7 Installs: 33 Source: obra/superpowers Description: Use when completing tasks, implementing major features, or before merging to verify work meets requirements

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#8): executing-plans
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #8 Installs: 33 Source: obra/superpowers Description: Use when you have a written implementation plan to execute in a separate session with review checkpoints

item.title: [Trending] New entry (#44): azure-messaging
item.description: New entry in Trending leaderboard. Rank: #44 Installs: 1305 Source: microsoft/github-copilot-for-azure Description: Troubleshoot and resolve issues with Azure Messaging SDKs for Event Hubs and Service Bus. Covers connection failures, authentication errors, message processing issues, and SDK configuration problems. USE FOR: event hub SDK error, service bus SDK issue, messaging connection failure, AMQP error, event processor host issue, message lock lost, send timeout, receiver disconnected, SDK troubleshooting, azure messaging SDK, event hub consumer, service bus queue issue, topic subscription error, enable logging event hub, service bus logging, eventhub python, servicebus java, eventhub javascript, servicebus dotnet, event hub checkpoint, event hub not receiving messages, service bus dead letter DO NOT USE FOR: creating Event Hub or Service Bus resources (use azure-prepare), monitoring metrics (use azure-observability), cost analysis (use azure-cost-optimization)
